free_text: The 50Cr(a,n)53Fe and 50Cr(a,p)53Mn cross sections
5U Pelletron
A metallic chromium target, enriched to 96.75% in 50Cr,
was prepared by evaporation from an intimate mixture of
Cr2O3 and powdered tantalum which acted as a reducing
agent. The determinations of target thickness agreed at
the 5% level. The adopted value was 2.45*10**18 50Cr
atoms*cm**(-2).
Data were received from the author: S.G.Tims
and from table 2.
LVL-NUMB changed on E-LVL in sub.004,005
Data received from the author: Dr S.G.Tims
A 120 cm**3 Ge(Li) detector was located 2 cm
from the target in the 55 angle direction.
For neutron counting measurements the target
chamber was a stainless steel tubular cross located
at the center on the neutron detector which consisted
of 32x32x44 cm3 paraffin castle embedded in which
were six 3He-filled proportional counters.
neutron counting by 4PI neutron detector
Statistical error
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors being gamma-ray detector efficiency - 8%,
target thickness (5%), beam  current integration (3%).
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors being neutron detector efficiency - 12%,
target thickness (5%), beam current integration (3%).
200 mm**2 surface barrier detector mounted
8.6 cm from the target in the 125 angle direction
and covered with a 50 mu-m Al foil
Digitizing error
Scale error. The main contributors being
* target thickness 5%,
* the solid angle and beam-current integration 8%,
* angular-distribution corrections 3%.
